meta.ministry: Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions, Department of Administrative Reforms and Public Grievances
meta.project_subject: Setting up of Project Monitoring Unit (PMU) for the works relating to the Implementation of recommendations of NeSDA 2021 - Extension thereof from 07.02.2024 to 31.03.2024
meta.deliverables: Progressive increase in number of mandatory services in States/ UTs/ Identified Ministries; Saturation of e-Services in States/ UTs/ Identified Ministries; Adoption of Unified Portal with special focus on North Eastern States for creating more services on Unified Portal by States/ UTs/ Identified Ministries; Improving State/ UT/ Identified Ministry Portal parameters in line with NeSDA 2023 by strengthening all seven focus parameters with special emphasis on Open Government Data (OGD), E-Participation and Leveraging Emerging Technologies; Integration of Service Plus, MyScheme and other such Portals of Meity with States/ UTs/ Identified Ministries portals for further strengthening them; Promotion of Delivery of faceless and suo-moto services by States/ UTs/ Identified Ministries; Improving of Identified Ministry Portals; Developing and Maintaining NeSDA Way Forward Portal to incorporate the above parameters; Any other related work assigned by the Department beyond the above parameters from time to time.

content.full_text: This work order extends the engagement of Quality Council of India for setting up a Project Monitoring Unit (PMU) to support implementation of NeSDA 2021 recommendations. The extension runs from 07.02.2024 to 31.03.2024 at a cost of Rs. 9,57,600 plus applicable taxes. The Department of Administrative Reforms and Public Grievances under the Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions issued this order. The scope includes expanding mandatory e-services across States/UTs/Ministries, saturating e-Service availability, promoting Unified Portal adoption especially in North Eastern States, aligning portals with NeSDA 2023 parameters focusing on Open Government Data, E-Participation and emerging technologies, integrating central portals like Service Plus and MyScheme with state/ministry portals, promoting faceless and suo-moto service delivery, improving ministry portals, and maintaining the NeSDA Way Forward Portal. Payment is milestone-based: 25% on award and commencement, 75% on completion of tenure.
